docs.title: FHIRLab Core
docs.text:
A minimal, Docker-based FHIR learning environment for healthcare teams

FHIRLab Core provides everything you need to start learning and experimenting with FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) - without requiring DevOps expertise.

  • Quick Start

    Get running in under 10 minutes with just Docker installed.

    Get started

  • HAPI FHIR R4

    Full-featured FHIR R4 server for storing and querying healthcare data.

    Learn more

  • SNOMED CT

    Snowstorm terminology server with visual browser for clinical coding.

    Explore

  • Postman Collection

    Ready-to-use API examples for HAPI FHIR and Snowstorm.

    Import collection

  • Troubleshooting

    Common issues and how to resolve them.

    Get help

docs.title: What's Included
docs.text: Service Description Default Port HAPI FHIR FHIR R4 server for healthcare data 8080 Snowstorm SNOMED CT terminology server 8081 SNOMED Browser Visual interface for SNOMED CT 8082 SMART Launcher (Optional) Separate SMART launcher service 8083

docs.title: Who Is This For?
docs.text:
FHIRLab Core is designed for:

  • Healthcare IT teams exploring FHIR integration
  • Developers building FHIR-enabled applications
  • Educators teaching healthcare interoperability
  • Students learning about modern health data standards

Learning Environment

FHIRLab Core is designed for learning and development, not production use. It prioritizes simplicity over scalability and security hardening.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Minimum Recommended RAM 8 GB 16 GB Disk Space 20 GB 50 GB Docker 20.10+ Latest Docker Compose 2.0+ Latest

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Check the Troubleshooting guide
  • Review Upstream Documentation for detailed reference
  • Open an issue on GitHub

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
FormLab is a comprehensive SMART-on-FHIR learning environment that provides hands-on experience with healthcare forms, questionnaires, and SMART application launch sequences. It goes beyond basic SMART authentication to demonstrate real-world clinical workflows using Structured Data Capture (SDC) questionnaires.

FormLab is developed and maintained by the Australian e-Health Research Centre (AEHRC).

Reference: FormLab GitLab Repository

docs.title: What FormLab Provides
docs.text:
FormLab includes a complete stack of services:

  • Interactive Homepage - Documentation and overview of the environment
  • SMART Launcher UI - Web-based interface for launching SMART applications
  • Forms Application - SDC questionnaire renderer and form management tool
  • HAPI FHIR Server - Dedicated FHIR R4 server pre-loaded with sample data
  • PostgreSQL Database - Persistent storage for FHIR resources
  • Sample Data Loader - Automatically populates the FHIR server with example patients, questionnaires, and clinical data

docs.title: Why Use FormLab?
docs.text:
• Learn SMART on FHIR - Experience the complete SMART launch workflow in a realistic setting
  • Explore SDC Questionnaires - Work with healthcare forms and structured data capture
  • Practice with Sample Data - Pre-loaded clinical scenarios for immediate experimentation
  • Understand Integration - See how SMART apps, FHIR servers, and forms work together
  • No Configuration Required - Everything is pre-configured and ready to use

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• FHIRLab Core installed (see Quick Start)
  • At least 2GB additional RAM available
  • Ports 8084-8088 available on your system

docs.title: Enable FormLab
docs.text:
FormLab is enabled using Docker Compose profiles:

cd docker
docker compose --profile formlab up -d

This starts all FormLab services in the background. Initial startup may take 2-3 minutes as services initialize and sample data is loaded.

docs.title: Verify FormLab is Running
docs.text:
Check that all services are healthy:

docker compose ps

You should see FormLab services listed with status "Up" or "healthy": - fhirlab-formlab-homepage - fhirlab-formlab-launcher - fhirlab-formlab-proxy - fhirlab-formlab-forms - fhirlab-formlab-fhir - fhirlab-formlab-db

docs.title: Access FormLab
docs.text:
Open your web browser and navigate to:

http://localhost:8084

This opens the FormLab homepage with links to all components and documentation.

docs.title: Basic Workflow
docs.text:
1. Start at the Homepage (http://localhost:8084)
  2. Review the overview and available components

  3. Access documentation and guides

  4. Launch a SMART Application (http://localhost:8085)

  5. Select a patient from the launcher
  6. Choose the Forms Application

  7. Click "Launch" to initiate the SMART sequence

  8. Work with Forms (http://localhost:8087)

  9. Browse available questionnaires
  10. Fill out forms with patient data

  11. Submit QuestionnaireResponse resources to the FHIR server

  12. Query FHIR Resources (http://localhost:8088/fhir)

  13. Access the dedicated FormLab FHIR server
  14. Query for Patients, Questionnaires, QuestionnaireResponses
  15. Explore the sample data

docs.title: Component URLs
docs.text: Component URL Purpose Homepage http://localhost:8084 Documentation and entry point SMART Launcher http://localhost:8085 Launch SMART applications SMART Proxy http://localhost:8086 SMART authentication proxy Forms Application http://localhost:8087 SDC questionnaire renderer FHIR Server http://localhost:8088/fhir Dedicated FHIR R4 API

docs.title: Integration with Core Services
docs.text:
FormLab runs as a standalone environment with its own FHIR server and database, separate from the core HAPI FHIR service. This design allows you to:

  • Experiment with FormLab without affecting core FHIRLab data
  • Compare different FHIR server setups
  • Learn how complete healthcare IT stacks are architected
  • Reset FormLab data independently

If you want to connect FormLab to the core HAPI FHIR server instead, see Advanced Configuration.

docs.title: Combining with Other Profiles
docs.text:
You can run FormLab alongside other optional services:

# Run FormLab and basic SMART launcher together
docker compose --profile formlab --profile smart up -d

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
FormLab configuration can be customized in docker/.env:

# FormLab Ports
FORMLAB_PORT=8084                 # Homepage
FORMLAB_LAUNCHER_PORT=8085        # SMART Launcher UI
FORMLAB_PROXY_PORT=8086           # SMART Proxy
FORMLAB_FORMS_PORT=8087           # Forms Application
FORMLAB_FHIR_PORT=8088            # FHIR Server

# FormLab Database
FORMLAB_DB_USER=admin
FORMLAB_DB_PASSWORD=admin
FORMLAB_DB_NAME=hapi

docs.title: Port Conflicts
docs.text:
If you have services already using these ports, modify the environment variables:

# Example: Move FormLab to higher port range
FORMLAB_PORT=9000
FORMLAB_LAUNCHER_PORT=9001
FORMLAB_PROXY_PORT=9002
FORMLAB_FORMS_PORT=9003
FORMLAB_FHIR_PORT=9004

After changing ports, restart the services:

docker compose --profile formlab down
docker compose --profile formlab up -d

docs.title: FormLab Services Won't Start
docs.text:
Symptom: Services fail to start or exit immediately

Solutions: 1. Check port availability: 

# On Linux/Mac
lsof -i :8084
lsof -i :8085
# etc.

  1. Check Docker resources:
  2. Ensure at least 6GB RAM total (4GB for core + 2GB for FormLab)

  3. Increase Docker memory allocation if needed

  4. View service logs: 

    docker compose logs formlab-homepage
docker compose logs formlab-fhir

docs.title: Cannot Connect to FormLab Services
docs.text:
Symptom: Browser shows "connection refused" or "cannot connect"

Solutions: 1. Verify services are running: 

docker compose ps | grep formlab

  1. Check service health: 

    docker compose logs formlab-homepage

  2. Ensure you're using the correct URL (http://localhost:8084, not https)

  3. Try accessing the FHIR server directly: 

    curl http://localhost:8088/fhir/metadata

docs.title: Sample Data Not Loading
docs.text:
Symptom: FHIR server is empty, no patients or questionnaires

Solutions: 1. Check if the sample data loader ran: 

docker compose logs formlab-samples

  1. The sample loader runs once on startup. If it failed, restart it: 

    docker compose --profile formlab down
docker compose --profile formlab up -d

  2. Wait 2-3 minutes for all services to fully initialize

docs.title: FormLab FHIR Server Connection Issues
docs.text:
Symptom: Forms app cannot connect to FHIR server

Solutions: 1. Verify FHIR server is accessible: 

curl http://localhost:8088/fhir/metadata

  1. Check proxy configuration: 

    docker compose logs formlab-proxy

  2. Ensure all services are on the same Docker network: 

    docker network inspect fhirlab-network

docs.title: Database Errors
docs.text:
Symptom: FHIR server shows database connection errors

Solutions: 1. Check PostgreSQL is running: 

docker compose logs formlab-db

  1. Reset the database: 

    docker compose down -v
docker compose --profile formlab up -d

  2. Verify database credentials in .env match configuration

docs.title: Stopping FormLab
docs.text:
To stop FormLab services while keeping core services running:

docker compose --profile formlab down

To stop all services:

docker compose down

docs.title: Resetting FormLab Data
docs.text:
To completely reset FormLab and start fresh:

# Stop and remove all volumes
docker compose --profile formlab down -v

# Start again
docker compose --profile formlab up -d

This removes all FormLab data including the PostgreSQL database. Sample data will be reloaded automatically.

docs.title: Connecting FormLab to Core HAPI FHIR
docs.text:
By default, FormLab uses its own FHIR server. To connect FormLab forms to the core HAPI FHIR server:

  1. Modify docker/docker-compose.yml in the formlab-proxy service: 

    environment:
  - FHIR_SERVER_R4=http://hapi-fhir:8080/fhir

  2. Update the formlab-ehr-config to point to the core FHIR server

  3. Restart FormLab: 

    docker compose --profile formlab down
docker compose --profile formlab up -d

Note: This configuration is not officially supported and may require additional adjustments.

docs.title: Learning Resources
docs.text:
• FormLab GitLab Repository - Source code and documentation
  • SMART on FHIR - Official SMART specification
  • HL7 SDC Implementation Guide - Structured Data Capture standard
  • SMART Forms Documentation - Forms application details

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Explore sample questionnaires and patient data
  • Experiment with creating custom questionnaires
  • Practice the SMART launch sequence with different patients
  • Query FormLab's FHIR server using Postman or code
  • Compare FormLab's architecture with core FHIRLab services

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get FHIRLab Core running in under 10 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure you have:

  • Docker installed (Get Docker)
  • Docker Compose v2+ (included with Docker Desktop)
  • At least 8 GB RAM available
  • Basic command-line familiarity

Check Docker Installation

Run docker --version and docker compose version to verify your installation.

docs.title: Step 1: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/fhirlab/core.git
cd core

docs.title: Step 2: Start the Services
docs.text:
cd docker
./scripts/start.sh

This will:

  1. Create a default configuration file (.env)
  2. Pull the required Docker images
  3. Start all services
  4. Wait for services to become healthy

First Run

The first start takes longer as Docker downloads the images (~2-3 GB). Subsequent starts are much faster.

docs.title: Step 3: Verify Everything Works
docs.text:
./scripts/smoke-test.sh

You should see output like:

========================================
  FHIRLab Core - Smoke Test
========================================

Core Services:
  HAPI FHIR Server          PASS
  HAPI FHIR R4 Version      PASS
  Elasticsearch             PASS
  Snowstorm                 PASS
  SNOMED Browser            PASS

========================================
  Summary
========================================

  Passed:   5
  Failed:   0
  Warnings: 0

All critical tests passed!

docs.title: Step 4: Explore the Services
docs.text:
Open these URLs in your browser:

Service URL Description HAPI FHIR http://localhost:8080 FHIR server web interface FHIR API http://localhost:8080/fhir FHIR REST API endpoint Snowstorm http://localhost:8081 Terminology server API SNOMED Browser http://localhost:8082 Visual SNOMED CT browser

docs.title: Step 5: Load Example Data (Optional)
docs.text:
To populate the FHIR server with example resources:

./scripts/load-data.sh

No Installation Required

The script uses the HAPI FHIR CLI bundled inside the Docker container.

Data Persistence

HAPI FHIR uses an in-memory database by default. Data will be cleared when containers restart. Run load-data.sh again after restarts to reload example data. This is intentional for a learning environment.

docs.title: What's Next?
docs.text:
• Postman Collection - Ready-to-use API examples
  • Using the API - Query and create FHIR resources
  • Loading Data - Import your own data
  • Configuration - Customize ports and settings

Try the Postman Collection

Import the ready-to-use Postman collection from postman/FHIRLab-Core.postman_collection.json to explore all API endpoints with pre-configured examples. See the Postman Collection guide for details.

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Command Description ./scripts/start.sh Start all services ./scripts/stop.sh Stop all services (data preserved) ./scripts/reset.sh Stop and delete all data ./scripts/smoke-test.sh Verify services are healthy ./scripts/load-data.sh Load example FHIR resources

docs.title: Enable SMART Launcher (Optional)
docs.text:
To include the SMART launcher service for learning (runs as a separate service, not a HAPI mode):

./scripts/start.sh --smart

Then access the SMART Launcher at http://localhost:8083.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Solutions for common issues with FHIRLab Core.

docs.title: Port Already in Use
docs.text:
Error: Bind for 0.0.0.0:8080 failed: port is already allocated

Solution:

  1. Find what's using the port: 

    # Linux/macOS
lsof -i :8080

# Windows
netstat -ano | findstr :8080

  2. Either stop the conflicting service, or change the port in .env: 

    HAPI_FHIR_PORT=9080

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text:
Symptoms:

  • Containers keep restarting
  • docker logs shows OutOfMemoryError
  • System becomes unresponsive

Solution:

  1. Check available memory: 

    docker stats

  2. Reduce memory allocation in .env: 

    ES_HEAP_SIZE=1g
ES_MEMORY=1g
SNOWSTORM_MAX_HEAP=1g

  3. Stop unused applications on your system

docs.title: Containers Won't Start
docs.text:
Check the logs:

docker compose logs --tail 50

Common causes:

Log Message Solution No space left on device Clear Docker storage: docker system prune Permission denied Check file permissions, run with correct user Network error Restart Docker daemon

docs.title: Snowstorm Takes Forever to Start
docs.text:
Expected: Snowstorm can take 2-5 minutes to fully initialize.

If it never starts:

  1. Check Elasticsearch is healthy first: 

    curl http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health

  2. Check Snowstorm logs: 

    docker compose logs snowstorm --tail 100

  3. Ensure sufficient memory is allocated

docs.title: HAPI FHIR Returns 500 Errors
docs.text:
Check the logs:

docker compose logs hapi-fhir --tail 100

Common causes:

  • Database corruption (try reset.sh)
  • Invalid FHIR resources being submitted
  • Memory pressure

docs.title: Snowstorm Search Returns No Results
docs.text:
If SNOMED content wasn't loaded:

  1. Snowstorm works without content but returns empty results
  2. Load SNOMED CT RF2 files (requires license)
  3. See SNOMED Browser Guide

docs.title: Cannot Connect to Services
docs.text:
Verify services are running:

docker compose ps

Check network connectivity:

curl -v http://localhost:8080/fhir/metadata

Firewall issues:

  • Ensure Docker ports aren't blocked
  • On Linux, check iptables or firewalld
  • On Windows, check Windows Firewall

docs.title: Lost Data After Restart
docs.text:
Data should persist. If it didn't:

  1. Check you didn't run reset.sh
  2. Verify volumes exist: 
    docker volume ls | grep fhirlab
  3. Ensure you're using stop.sh, not docker compose down -v

docs.title: Cannot Delete Resources
docs.text:
HAPI FHIR requires explicit delete permission:

This is enabled by default in FHIRLab Core. If you've customized:

# Check configuration
docker compose exec hapi-fhir env | grep DELETE

docs.title: Database Corrupted
docs.text:
Symptoms:

  • Startup failures mentioning database
  • Missing data after restart
  • Inconsistent query results

Solution:

./scripts/reset.sh
./scripts/start.sh
./scripts/load-data.sh  # Reload example data

docs.title: Slow Queries
docs.text:
For large datasets:

  1. Add search parameters to narrow results: 

    # Instead of
curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/Observation

# Use
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Observation?subject=Patient/123&_count=100"

  2. Increase memory allocation

  3. Consider this is a learning environment, not production

docs.title: High CPU Usage
docs.text:
Check which container:

docker stats

Common causes:

  • Elasticsearch indexing (temporary)
  • Large data import
  • Insufficient memory causing swapping

docs.title: Collect Diagnostic Information
docs.text:
# System info
docker version
docker compose version

# Container status
docker compose ps

# Recent logs
docker compose logs --tail 100 > fhirlab-logs.txt

docs.title: Where to Get Help
docs.text:
1. Check this troubleshooting guide
  2. Review Upstream Documentation
  3. Search existing issues on GitHub
  4. Open a new issue with diagnostic information

docs.title: Full Reset
docs.text:
When all else fails:

./scripts/reset.sh --force
docker system prune -a  # Caution: removes ALL Docker data
./scripts/start.sh

docs.title: Restart Docker
docs.text:
Sometimes Docker itself needs a restart:

# macOS/Windows: Restart Docker Desktop

# Linux
sudo systemctl restart docker

docs.title: Using the API
docs.text: Learn how to interact with the HAPI FHIR REST API.

docs.title: API Endpoint
docs.text:
The FHIR R4 API is available at:

http://localhost:8080/fhir

docs.title: Server Capabilities
docs.text:
View the server's capability statement:

curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/metadata

This returns a CapabilityStatement resource describing supported features.

docs.title: Search Resources
docs.text:
List all patients:

curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient

Search with parameters:

# Find patients named "Smith"
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient?family=Smith"

# Find observations with a specific code
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Observation?code=http://loinc.org|55284-4"

docs.title: Read a Resource
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient/123

docs.title: Create a Resource
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient \
  -H "Content-Type: application/fhir+json" \
  -d '{
    "resourceType": "Patient",
    "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["John"]}],
    "birthDate": "1980-01-15"
  }'

docs.title: Update a Resource
docs.text:
curl -X PUT http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient/123 \
  -H "Content-Type: application/fhir+json" \
  -d '{
    "resourceType": "Patient",
    "id": "123",
    "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["John", "Robert"]}],
    "birthDate": "1980-01-15"
  }'

docs.title: Delete a Resource
docs.text: curl -X DELETE http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient/123

docs.title: Common Search Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Example _id Resource ID ?_id=123 _lastUpdated Last update time ?_lastUpdated=gt2023-01-01 _count Results per page ?_count=50 _sort Sort order ?_sort=-_lastUpdated _summary Summary mode ?_summary=count

docs.title: Patient-Specific
docs.text: Parameter Description Example family Family name ?family=Smith given Given name ?given=John birthdate Birth date ?birthdate=1980-01-15 identifier Identifier ?identifier=12345

docs.title: Chaining and Includes
docs.text:
# Find observations for a specific patient
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Observation?subject=Patient/123"

# Include referenced resources
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Observation?_include=Observation:subject"

docs.title: JSON (Default)
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient \
  -H "Accept: application/fhir+json"

docs.title: XML
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient \
  -H "Accept: application/fhir+xml"

docs.title: Pretty Print
docs.text:
Add _pretty=true to format the response:

curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient?_pretty=true"

docs.title: Transaction Bundle
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/fhir \
  -H "Content-Type: application/fhir+json" \
  -d '{
    "resourceType": "Bundle",
    "type": "transaction",
    "entry": [
      {
        "resource": {
          "resourceType": "Patient",
          "name": [{"family": "Doe"}]
        },
        "request": {
          "method": "POST",
          "url": "Patient"
        }
      }
    ]
  }'

docs.title: Bulk Export
docs.text:
# Initiate export
curl -X GET "http://localhost:8080/fhir/\$export" \
  -H "Accept: application/fhir+json" \
  -H "Prefer: respond-async"

docs.title: Web Interface
docs.text:
HAPI FHIR includes a web interface for browsing and testing:

  1. Open http://localhost:8080
  2. Use the search forms to query resources
  3. View and edit resources through the UI

docs.title: Using Postman
docs.text: See the Postman Collection for a pre-built collection of API requests.

docs.title: Loading Data
docs.text: Learn how to load FHIR resources and terminology into FHIRLab Core.

docs.title: Loading Example FHIR Resources
docs.text:
The load-data.sh script uploads example FHIR R4 resources to the server:

./scripts/load-data.sh

This loads a variety of example resources including:

  • Patients
  • Practitioners
  • Organizations
  • Observations
  • Conditions
  • Medications
  • And more...

No Installation Required

The script uses the HAPI FHIR CLI bundled inside the Docker container. No local installation is needed.

Reference: HAPI FHIR CLI Documentation

docs.title: Uploading Terminology
docs.text:
Upload custom terminology files (CodeSystems, ValueSets):

./scripts/upload-terminology.sh <path-to-file>

The script copies your file into the Docker container and uses the bundled HAPI FHIR CLI to upload it.

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text:
• CodeSystem resources (JSON or XML)
  • ValueSet resources (JSON or XML)
  • FHIR Bundles containing terminology resources

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Upload a custom CodeSystem
./scripts/upload-terminology.sh ./my-codesystem.json

# Upload a ValueSet
./scripts/upload-terminology.sh ./my-valueset.xml

Reference: HAPI FHIR CLI - Upload Terminology

docs.title: Loading Data via API
docs.text: You can also load data directly using the FHIR REST API.

docs.title: Create a Single Resource
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient \
  -H "Content-Type: application/fhir+json" \
  -d '{
    "resourceType": "Patient",
    "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["John"]}]
  }'

docs.title: Upload a Bundle
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/fhir \
  -H "Content-Type: application/fhir+json" \
  -d @my-bundle.json

docs.title: Loading SNOMED CT Content
docs.text: Snowstorm requires SNOMED CT RF2 release files. Due to licensing requirements, these are not included.

docs.title: Obtaining SNOMED CT Files
docs.text:
1. Register at SNOMED International
  2. Download the RF2 release files
  3. Follow the Snowstorm import documentation

Licensing

SNOMED CT usage requires appropriate licensing. FHIRLab Core uses Snowstorm to avoid licensing issues with the server itself, but content still requires proper licensing.

docs.title: Check FHIR Resources
docs.text:
# List all patients
curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/Patient

# Count observations
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/Observation?_summary=count"

docs.title: Check Terminology
docs.text:
# List CodeSystems
curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/CodeSystem

# List ValueSets
curl http://localhost:8080/fhir/ValueSet

docs.title: Clearing Data
docs.text:
To remove all data and start fresh:

./scripts/reset.sh

This removes all FHIR resources, terminology, and Elasticsearch data.

docs.title: Starting & Stopping
docs.text: Learn how to manage FHIRLab Core services.

docs.title: Basic Start
docs.text:
Start the core services (HAPI FHIR, Snowstorm, SNOMED Browser):

cd docker
./scripts/start.sh

The script will:

  1. Check for .env file (creates from .env.example if missing)
  2. Start all Docker containers
  3. Wait for each service to become healthy
  4. Display service URLs when ready

docs.title: Start with SMART Launcher
docs.text:
Include the optional SMART launcher service (separate service that connects to HAPI):

./scripts/start.sh --smart

docs.title: Manual Start (Alternative)
docs.text:
If you prefer using Docker Compose directly:

# Core services only
docker compose up -d

# With SMART launcher (separate service)
docker compose --profile smart up -d

docs.title: Graceful Stop
docs.text:
Stop all services while preserving data:

./scripts/stop.sh

This is equivalent to:

docker compose --profile smart down

Your data remains in Docker volumes and will be available when you restart.

docs.title: Interactive Reset
docs.text:
Remove all data and start fresh:

./scripts/reset.sh

The script will:

  1. Ask for confirmation
  2. Stop all services
  3. Remove all data volumes
  4. Leave you ready for a fresh start

docs.title: Force Reset (No Confirmation)
docs.text:
Skip the confirmation prompt:

./scripts/reset.sh --force

Data Loss

Reset permanently deletes all FHIR resources, terminology data, and configurations. This cannot be undone.

docs.title: Smoke Test
docs.text:
Verify all services are running correctly:

./scripts/smoke-test.sh

For SMART launcher testing:

./scripts/smoke-test.sh --smart

docs.title: Docker Status
docs.text:
View container status:

docker compose ps

View logs:

# All services
docker compose logs

# Specific service
docker compose logs hapi-fhir

# Follow logs in real-time
docker compose logs -f

docs.title: Service Startup Times
docs.text:
Service Typical Startup Time HAPI FHIR 30-60 seconds Elasticsearch 30-60 seconds Snowstorm 1-3 minutes SNOMED Browser 10-20 seconds SMART Launcher 10-20 seconds

Patience with Snowstorm

Snowstorm can take several minutes to fully initialize, especially on first run or after loading terminology data.

docs.title: Services Won't Start
docs.text:
1. Check available memory: docker stats
  2. Verify ports aren't in use: netstat -tlnp | grep 808
  3. Review logs: docker compose logs

docs.title: Health Checks Failing
docs.text:
1. Wait longer - some services need time
  2. Check memory allocation in .env
  3. See Troubleshooting for specific errors

docs.title: Container Keeps Restarting
docs.text:
# Check why a container is restarting
docker compose logs hapi-fhir --tail 50

Common causes:

  • Insufficient memory
  • Port conflict
  • Configuration error

docs.title: SNOMED CT Browser
docs.text: Explore SNOMED CT terminology using the visual browser.

docs.title: Accessing the Browser
docs.text: Open http://localhost:8082 in your web browser.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The SNOMED CT Browser provides a visual interface for:

  • Browsing the SNOMED CT hierarchy
  • Searching for clinical concepts
  • Viewing concept details and relationships
  • Exploring reference sets

docs.title: Loading SNOMED CT Content
docs.text:
Content Not Included

SNOMED CT content is not included due to licensing requirements. You'll need to load content separately.

docs.title: Obtaining SNOMED CT
docs.text:
1. Register at SNOMED International Member Licensing
  2. Download the appropriate RF2 release files
  3. Import using Snowstorm's import API

docs.title: Import Process
docs.text:
Refer to the Snowstorm documentation for detailed import instructions.

Basic import via API:

# Start import job
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8081/imports" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "branchPath": "MAIN",
    "createCodeSystemVersion": true,
    "type": "SNAPSHOT"
  }'

docs.title: Snowstorm API
docs.text: The Snowstorm API is available at http://localhost:8081.

docs.title: Swagger Documentation
docs.text: View interactive API docs at http://localhost:8081/swagger-ui.html.

docs.title: Search Concepts
docs.text: curl "http://localhost:8081/MAIN/concepts?term=diabetes"

docs.title: Get Concept Details
docs.text: curl "http://localhost:8081/MAIN/concepts/73211009"

docs.title: Find Children
docs.text: curl "http://localhost:8081/MAIN/concepts/73211009/children"

docs.title: Hierarchy Navigation
docs.text:
• Browse from the root of the SNOMED CT hierarchy
  • Expand/collapse concept trees
  • View parent and child relationships

docs.title: Concept Search
docs.text:
• Full-text search across descriptions
  • Filter by semantic tags
  • Search within specific reference sets

docs.title: Concept Details
docs.text:
View for each concept:

  • Fully Specified Name (FSN)
  • Preferred terms in different languages
  • Synonyms
  • Relationships (IS-A, defining relationships)
  • Reference set memberships

docs.title: Integration with HAPI FHIR
docs.text:
Snowstorm can be used as a terminology server for HAPI FHIR:

# Validate a code
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/CodeSystem/\$validate-code?url=http://snomed.info/sct&code=73211009"

# Expand a ValueSet
curl "http://localhost:8080/fhir/ValueSet/\$expand?url=http://snomed.info/sct?fhir_vs"

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• Snowstorm GitHub
  • SNOMED CT Browser Documentation
  • SNOMED International

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Customize FHIRLab Core for your environment.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Configuration is managed through the .env file in the docker/ directory.

docs.title: Creating Your Configuration
docs.text:
cd docker
cp .env.example .env

Edit .env to customize settings.

docs.title: HAPI FHIR Server
docs.text: Variable Default Description HAPI_FHIR_PORT 8080 Port for HAPI FHIR web interface and API HAPI_FHIR_SERVER_ADDRESS http://localhost:8080/fhir External URL for resource references

docs.title: Elasticsearch
docs.text: Variable Default Description ES_PORT 127.0.0.1:9200 Elasticsearch port (localhost only by default) ES_HEAP_SIZE 2g JVM heap size for Elasticsearch ES_MEMORY 2g Memory reservation for container

docs.title: Snowstorm
docs.text: Variable Default Description SNOWSTORM_PORT 8081 Port for Snowstorm API SNOWSTORM_MIN_HEAP 1g Minimum JVM heap SNOWSTORM_MAX_HEAP 2g Maximum JVM heap

docs.title: SNOMED Browser
docs.text: Variable Default Description SNOMED_BROWSER_PORT 8082 Port for SNOMED CT Browser

docs.title: SMART Launcher (Optional Separate Service)
docs.text:
The SMART launcher is a separate service that connects to HAPI FHIR. No HAPI-specific configuration needed.

Variable Default Description SMART_LAUNCHER_PORT 8083 Port for SMART Launcher service

docs.title: Change Default Ports
docs.text:
If port 8080 is already in use:

# In .env
HAPI_FHIR_PORT=9080
HAPI_FHIR_SERVER_ADDRESS=http://localhost:9080/fhir

docs.title: Reduce Memory Usage
docs.text:
For systems with limited RAM (8GB or less):

# In .env
ES_HEAP_SIZE=1g
ES_MEMORY=1g
SNOWSTORM_MIN_HEAP=512m
SNOWSTORM_MAX_HEAP=1g

docs.title: Allow Remote Access to Elasticsearch
docs.text:
Security Risk

Only do this in trusted environments.

# In .env
ES_PORT=0.0.0.0:9200

docs.title: External Server Address
docs.text:
If accessing from other machines:

# In .env
HAPI_FHIR_SERVER_ADDRESS=http://192.168.1.100:8080/fhir

docs.title: Docker Compose Overrides
docs.text:
For advanced customization, create docker-compose.override.yml:

services:
  hapi-fhir:
    environment:
      - hapi.fhir.openapi_enabled=true
      - hapi.fhir.cors.allowed_origin=*

This file is automatically merged with the main compose file.

docs.title: Data Locations
docs.text:
Service Storage Persistence HAPI FHIR In-memory H2 database Data resets on restart Snowstorm Docker volume fhirlab-elastic-data Persists between restarts

HAPI FHIR Data

By default, HAPI FHIR uses an in-memory database for simplicity. Data will be lost when the container restarts. This is intentional for a learning environment - use load-data.sh to reload example data after restart.

docs.title: Backup Volumes
docs.text:
# List volumes
docker volume ls | grep fhirlab

# Backup a volume
docker run --rm \
  -v fhirlab-hapi-data:/source:ro \
  -v $(pwd):/backup \
  alpine tar czf /backup/hapi-backup.tar.gz -C /source .

docs.title: Restore Volumes
docs.text:
docker run --rm \
  -v fhirlab-hapi-data:/target \
  -v $(pwd):/backup \
  alpine sh -c "cd /target && tar xzf /backup/hapi-backup.tar.gz"

docs.title: Network Configuration
docs.text: All services communicate on the fhirlab-network Docker network.

docs.title: Service Discovery
docs.text:
Within the Docker network, services can reach each other by name:

  • hapi-fhir:8080 - HAPI FHIR
  • elasticsearch:9200 - Elasticsearch
  • snowstorm:8080 - Snowstorm
  • snomed-browser:80 - SNOMED Browser

docs.title: Verify Environment
docs.text:
# Check loaded environment
docker compose config

# Verify specific variable
docker compose config | grep HAPI_FHIR_PORT

docs.title: Reset to Defaults
docs.text:
cp .env.example .env
./scripts/reset.sh
./scripts/start.sh

docs.title: Postman Collection
docs.text:
Use Postman to explore the FHIRLab Core APIs interactively.

Ready-to-Use Collection

FHIRLab Core includes a pre-configured Postman collection with 15 example requests for HAPI FHIR and Snowstorm APIs. No manual setup required!

docs.title: Download Collection Files
docs.text:
The collection files are included in the repository:

  • Collection: postman/FHIRLab-Core.postman_collection.json
  • Environment: postman/FHIRLab-Core.postman_environment.json

Or download directly:

  • Download Collection
  • Download Environment

docs.title: Getting Postman
docs.text: Download Postman from postman.com/downloads.

docs.title: Importing the Collection
docs.text: FHIRLab Core provides ready-to-use Postman collection and environment files.

docs.title: Quick Import
docs.text:
1. Open Postman
  2. Click Import in the top left
  3. Select Upload Files
  4. Import both files from the repository:
  5. postman/FHIRLab-Core.postman_collection.json - Request collection
  6. postman/FHIRLab-Core.postman_environment.json - Environment variables
  7. Select the "FHIRLab Core - Localhost" environment from the dropdown in the top right

You're ready to send requests! Try the "Get Server Metadata" request under HAPI FHIR → Server Metadata.

docs.title: What's Included
docs.text:
The collection includes 15 pre-configured requests organized into folders:

HAPI FHIR: - Server Metadata (CapabilityStatement) - Patient Operations (List, Search, Get, Create) - Observation Operations (List, Search by Patient, Search by Code) - Bundle Operations (Transaction Bundle)

Snowstorm: - Version & Health Check - Concept Search (with example: "diabetes") - Concept Details (Get concept, descriptions, children)

All requests include response validation tests and use environment variables for easy configuration.

docs.title: Manual Setup
docs.text: If you prefer to create the collection manually, follow the examples below.

docs.title: Environment Setup
docs.text:
The imported environment file includes these pre-configured variables for localhost deployment:

Variable Value Description fhir_base http://localhost:8080/fhir HAPI FHIR base URL snowstorm_base http://localhost:8081 Snowstorm base URL

docs.title: Server Metadata
docs.text:
GET {{fhir_base}}/metadata

Returns the server's CapabilityStatement.

docs.title: Patient Operations
docs.text:
List Patients: 

GET {{fhir_base}}/Patient

Search by Name: 

GET {{fhir_base}}/Patient?family=Smith

Get Patient by ID: 

GET {{fhir_base}}/Patient/123

Create Patient: 

POST {{fhir_base}}/Patient
Content-Type: application/fhir+json

{
  "resourceType": "Patient",
  "name": [
    {
      "family": "Smith",
      "given": ["John"]
    }
  ],
  "birthDate": "1980-01-15",
  "gender": "male"
}

docs.title: Observation Operations
docs.text:
List Observations: 

GET {{fhir_base}}/Observation

Search by Patient: 

GET {{fhir_base}}/Observation?subject=Patient/123

Search by Code: 

GET {{fhir_base}}/Observation?code=http://loinc.org|55284-4

docs.title: Bundle Operations
docs.text:
Transaction Bundle: 

POST {{fhir_base}}
Content-Type: application/fhir+json

{
  "resourceType": "Bundle",
  "type": "transaction",
  "entry": [
    {
      "resource": {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Doe"}]
      },
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "Patient"
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Version Info
docs.text: GET {{snowstorm_base}}/version

docs.title: Search Concepts
docs.text: GET {{snowstorm_base}}/MAIN/concepts?term=diabetes&limit=10

docs.title: Get Concept Details
docs.text: GET {{snowstorm_base}}/MAIN/concepts/73211009

docs.title: Get Concept Descriptions
docs.text: GET {{snowstorm_base}}/MAIN/concepts/73211009/descriptions

docs.title: Get Concept Children
docs.text: GET {{snowstorm_base}}/MAIN/concepts/73211009/children?form=inferred

docs.title: Save Responses as Examples
docs.text:
1. Send a request
  2. Click Save Response > Save as example
  3. Name it descriptively

docs.title: Use Pre-request Scripts
docs.text:
Set dynamic values:

pm.environment.set("timestamp", new Date().toISOString());

docs.title: Test Responses
docs.text:
Add tests to validate responses:

pm.test("Status code is 200", function () {
    pm.response.to.have.status(200);
});

pm.test("Response is FHIR Bundle", function () {
    var jsonData = pm.response.json();
    pm.expect(jsonData.resourceType).to.eql("Bundle");
});

docs.title: Collection Variables
docs.text:
Store commonly used values:

  • Patient IDs for testing
  • Code system URLs
  • ValueSet references

docs.title: External Resources
docs.text:
• HAPI FHIR Server Documentation
  • FHIR R4 Specification
  • Snowstorm API Documentation
  • Postman Learning Center

docs.title: Scripts Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all FHIRLab Core scripts.

docs.title: start.sh
docs.text:
Starts all FHIRLab Core services.

./scripts/start.sh [options]

Options:

Option Description --smart Also start the SMART launcher service (separate service)

Behavior:

  1. Creates .env from .env.example if missing
  2. Starts Docker containers
  3. Waits for health checks to pass
  4. Displays service URLs

Example:

# Start core services
./scripts/start.sh

# Start with SMART launcher (separate service)
./scripts/start.sh --smart

docs.title: stop.sh
docs.text:
Stops all running services, preserving data.

./scripts/stop.sh

Behavior:

  1. Stops all containers (including SMART profile)
  2. Preserves data in Docker volumes

docs.title: reset.sh
docs.text:
Stops services and removes all data.

./scripts/reset.sh [options]

Options:

Option Description --force Skip confirmation prompt

Behavior:

  1. Prompts for confirmation (unless --force)
  2. Stops all containers
  3. Removes Docker volumes
  4. Cleans up orphaned resources

Data Loss

This permanently deletes all data. Cannot be undone.

Example:

# Interactive reset
./scripts/reset.sh

# Force reset (no prompt)
./scripts/reset.sh --force

docs.title: load-data.sh
docs.text:
Loads example FHIR resources into HAPI FHIR.

./scripts/load-data.sh

Requirements:

  • HAPI FHIR CLI installed
  • HAPI FHIR server running

Behavior:

  1. Checks for HAPI FHIR CLI
  2. Waits for server to be ready
  3. Uploads example R4 resources

Reference: HAPI FHIR CLI upload-examples

docs.title: upload-terminology.sh
docs.text:
Uploads terminology files to HAPI FHIR.

./scripts/upload-terminology.sh <file>

Arguments:

Argument Description <file> Path to terminology file (JSON or XML)

Supported Formats:

  • CodeSystem resources
  • ValueSet resources
  • FHIR Bundles containing terminology

Example:

./scripts/upload-terminology.sh ./my-codesystem.json
./scripts/upload-terminology.sh ./valueset-bundle.xml

Reference: HAPI FHIR CLI upload-terminology

docs.title: smoke-test.sh
docs.text:
Verifies all services are running correctly.

./scripts/smoke-test.sh [options]

Options:

Option Description --smart Also test SMART Launcher

Checks Performed:

Service Test HAPI FHIR Fetches CapabilityStatement HAPI FHIR R4 Verifies FHIR version Elasticsearch Checks cluster health Snowstorm Fetches version info SNOMED Browser HTTP connectivity SMART Launcher HTTP connectivity (if --smart)

Exit Codes:

Code Meaning 0 All critical tests passed N N tests failed

Example:

# Test core services
./scripts/smoke-test.sh

# Include SMART launcher (separate service)
./scripts/smoke-test.sh --smart

docs.title: Script Locations
docs.text:
All scripts are in the docker/scripts/ directory:

docker/
└── scripts/
    ├── start.sh
    ├── stop.sh
    ├── reset.sh
    ├── load-data.sh
    ├── upload-terminology.sh
    └── smoke-test.sh

docs.title: Using Scripts from Any Directory
docs.text:
Scripts can be run from any directory using the full path:

/path/to/core/docker/scripts/start.sh

Or add to your PATH:

export PATH="/path/to/core/docker/scripts:$PATH"

docs.title: Exercises
docs.text:
1. Create a Patient - POST a new Patient resource
  2. Search Observations - Query for vital signs
  3. Build a Bundle - Create a transaction with multiple resources
  4. Validate Resources - Use the $validate operation
  5. Terminology Lookup - Query SNOMED CT codes

docs.title: Certifications
docs.text: Certification Organization HL7 FHIR Certification HL7 International SNOMED CT Foundation SNOMED International

docs.title: Recommended Learning Path
docs.text:
1. Week 1-2: FHIR Fundamentals
  2. Read FHIR overview
  3. Complete FHIR Drills exercises

  4. Create basic resources in FHIRLab Core

  5. Week 3-4: Deep Dive

  6. Explore specific resource types
  7. Learn search parameters

  8. Practice with Postman

  9. Week 5-6: Terminology

  10. Introduction to SNOMED CT
  11. Using terminology in FHIR

  12. Explore Snowstorm API

  13. Ongoing: Build Projects

  14. Create a simple FHIR client
  15. Build a SMART on FHIR app
  16. Contribute to open source projects

docs.title: Contributing Back
docs.text:
Found a bug or want to contribute to these projects?

  • Check each project's contribution guidelines
  • Report issues on their respective GitHub/GitLab repositories
  • Consider contributing documentation improvements

FHIRLab Core gratefully acknowledges the work of all these open-source projects.
